evals.name: oauth2-refresh-retry
evals.prompt: Need a Ktor HTTP client for our backend to call a third-party API. Requirements: OAuth2 with token refresh on 401, retry on 5xx with exponential backoff (max 3), JSON serialization via kotlinx.serialization, request/response logging at INFO. Show the HttpClient config, Auth plugin setup with refreshTokens, and a typed call function. Current Ktor version.
evals.expected_output: HttpClient { } config with Auth + bearer + refreshTokens lambda, HttpRequestRetry plugin with exponentialDelay + retryOnServerErrors, ContentNegotiation + json(), Logging plugin with LogLevel.INFO, suspending typed call returning data class.
assertions: Auth plugin installed with bearer { } and refreshTokens { } lambda
HttpRequestRetry plugin with retryOnServerErrors and exponentialDelay
Suspending typed function returning data class

evals.name: multi-tenant-custom-header
evals.prompt: We're building a B2B platform — every outgoing API call needs an `X-Tenant-Id` header tied to the current request's tenant context. Backend is Spring Boot Kotlin with coroutines. Show the Ktor client setup that pulls tenant id from a CoroutineContext element and injects the header for every request, plus error handling when the tenant context is missing.
evals.expected_output: Custom plugin or defaultRequest using coroutineContext element, currentCoroutineContext() to read tenant, header injected per request, missing-tenant case throws explicit exception.
assertions: Custom plugin or defaultRequest block injects X-Tenant-Id header
Tenant id read from CoroutineContext element (currentCoroutineContext)
Missing-tenant case throws explicit exception with clear message
Ktor 3.4.x and coroutines APIs used correctly
